subject: Re: Troubleshooting Warp Tomogram Reconstruction
body:
Hi Ben,

In general, streaking is expected because of the missing wedge(s), although 
I agree the difference between these 2 results seems too much. Warp doesn't 
consider the additional geometry adjustment from IMOD that you get when you 
flatten your lamella. Because of this, the alignment can be quite off, 
which can generate more streaking. Can you do the IMOD step without this 
extra adjustment?

I am considering switching to good old WBP for reconstruction as the more 
precise CTF correction of the current implementation is probably useless 
for what these reconstructions are usually used for.

The big dark blob is going to be annoying in any case and might be just 
maskable enough for a BoxNet model.

Cheers,
Dimitry

On Thursday, March 18, 2021 at 8:16:42 PM UTC+1 benjami...@gmail.com wrote:

> Hi all,
>
> I am trying to reconstruct tomograms with Warp, and seeing a lot of 
> artifacts relative to my diagnostic reconstructions with the same 
> alignments in imod. I am not really changing anything between the two - I 
> exclude the same tilts from patch tracking and reconstruction, check tilt 
> handedness, and reconstruct with `normalized` checked. 
>
> Here is what my IMOD reconstructions looks like (I've done tomogram 
> positioning so it is flattened out, but the result is similar before 
> positioning):
> [image: Screen Shot 2021-03-18 at 11.58.14 AM.png]
>
> And here is the same tomogram in warp (deconv to highlight the streaking 
> artifacts):
> [image: Screen Shot 2021-03-18 at 11.50.29 AM.png]
> This is a case with more tilt than usual (sagging lamella) but even with 
> more standard lamella tilt I often see these artifacts. Is there any way to 
> improve these results? I'd like to be able to use the same reconstruction 
> from Warp for both particle picking and for morphology analysis, and the 
> current artifacts I am seeing make doing the latter very difficult relative 
> to output from IMOD.
>
> Would be very interested to see if there are steps that I can do better in 
> IMOD or Warp to try to improve this!!
>
> Thanks,
> Ben
>
>
>
> Benjamin Barad, PhD
> Postdoctoral Associate
> Grotjahn Lab @ Scripps Research
> He/His/Him
>
